target.name: Mission Zero Technologies
target.founder: Nicholas Chadwick, Shiladitya Ghosh, Gaël Gobaille-Shaw

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 9 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Mission Zero Technologies is a Series A climate and energy company founded by Nicholas Chadwick, Shiladitya Ghosh, and Gaël Gobaille-Shaw, having raised £21.8M+ to date. The target operates within a fragmented frontier-tech sector where public comparables are limited to a single listed peer (LNZA), while private benchmarks include eight companies across varying stages and technology vectors—notably Helion, Redwood Materials, QuantumScape, KoBold Metals, and Electric Hydrogen.

Standard peer-median valuation methodology is not applicable here. The sector contains nine Climate & Energy companies in aggregate, but none satisfy the filtering criteria for reliable triangulation: zero same-stage peers, zero adjacent-stage peers, and zero similar-valuation-tier peers exist with sufficient density. A forced all-sector median would introduce substantial noise given the heterogeneity in business models, capital intensity, and development timelines across frontier energy technologies. The single public comparable provides limited additional signal for a pre-revenue or early-revenue Series A asset.

The analysis indicates that conventional comps-based valuation frameworks lack sufficient precision for Mission Zero. Defensible valuation would require alternative methods: bottom-up discounted cash flow modeling with explicit technology adoption and commercialization timelines, venture capital method calibration to sector IRR expectations, or strategic acquirer assessment. Key uncertainties remain the company's specific technology position within Climate & Energy, unit economics pathway, and capital requirements to Series B. A range cannot be responsibly stated without granular operational data.
